---
title: "Lectures"
---

Each lecture comes in **two forms**, both built from the course library
[`libdpy`](https://github.com/applied-dp-course/pub_lib):

- a **presentation deck** — a notebook-first RevealJS slideshow with the prose trimmed to bullets
  and code hidden (each code slide is marked, and the code lives in the self-study notebook); and
- a **self-study notebook** — the full narrative plus all code, meant to be read and run.

## Available lectures

<!-- BEGIN AUTO-GENERATED LECTURES -->
- **02 · Reconstruction Attacks** — [presentation deck](lectures/02-reconstruction/slides.qmd) · [self-study notebook](lectures/02-reconstruction/notebook.ipynb)
- **03 · DP as Hypothesis Testing** — [presentation deck](lectures/03-hypothesis_testing/slides.qmd) · [self-study notebook](lectures/03-hypothesis_testing/learn.ipynb)
- **03b · DP as Hypothesis Testing** — [presentation deck](lectures/03b-hypothesis_testing/slides.qmd) · [self-study notebook](lectures/03b-hypothesis_testing/learn.ipynb)
<!-- END AUTO-GENERATED LECTURES -->

See the [schedule](schedule.qmd) for migration status, and [STATUS.md](STATUS.md) for live build progress.
